title: TIP-1.0 Compatibility Declaration
description: How a component declares its TIP version range and peer capability requirements. Included by every TIP manifest. Evaluated by core/contracts/compatibility.py during connection negotiation. See https://tokenpak.ai/protocol/compatibility.

tip_version_range.description: Version range the component can speak. Format: comma-separated version predicates, each '<op><TIP-MAJOR.MINOR>' where op is one of '>=', '>', '<=', '<', '==', '!='. Example: '>=TIP-1.0,<TIP-2.0' means 'TIP-1.0 and later, but not TIP-2.x'.

requires_peer_capabilities.description: Capability labels the peer MUST publish for the connection to succeed. Missing any = connection MUST fail with tip.capability.missing-required error (docs/protocol/compatibility.md §2).

optional_peer_capabilities.description: Capability labels the component will use if the peer publishes them, but does not require.

requires_profile.description: Profile identifier(s) the peer must advertise. E.g. an adapter's manifest may require the peer be a tip-proxy.

deprecated_since.description: Set when this component is deprecated starting at a given TIP MINOR. Consumers SHOULD warn on use; a subsequent MAJOR bump MAY refuse the connection.

removed_at.description: Set when the deprecation window closes. A component advertising removed_at <= current_tip_version MUST NOT connect.
